narrative.headline: 2018 Honda CR-V: engine and braking-assist complaints lead the pack
narrative.summary: Engine stalling, brake failure, and fuel-system contamination are the most commonly reported problems among owners of this CR-V generation. A large share of complaints also involve the automatic forward-collision braking system activating unexpectedly, sometimes triggering crashes, and the steering and driver-assistance systems going offline together. Taken together, the complaints cluster around powertrain reliability and the behavior of the electronic safety systems.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report the engine stalling without warning while driving, accompanied by brake failure and illumination of the ABS indicator light. · 1 fire · 4 crash · 3 injured

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: Owners report the automatic braking feature activating when no object is present ahead of the vehicle, with a brake warning displayed on the control panel. · 4 crash · 10 injured

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: Owners report discovering a significant amount of gasoline contaminating the engine oil reservoir after normal use and within a short period of ownership. · 5 crash · 3 injured

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: Owners report the cruise control, lane departure, and vehicle distance control systems all failing simultaneously, leaving the vehicle out of service for extended periods. · 5 crash · 3 injured

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 269 complaints (13%). Owners report dead or drained batteries, including no-start after the car sits parked. Linked to 5 crashes and 3 injuries.
